nodes.description: Dopaminergic nigrostriatal dysfunction and substantia nigra degeneration drive levodopa-responsive parkinsonism, bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor, dystonia, and postural instability.

evidence.reference_title: Dissecting the Phenotype and Genotype of PLA2G6-Related Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Presynaptic dopaminergic terminal imaging was abnormal in all where performed.

evidence.reference_title: PARK14 (D331Y) PLA2G6 Causes Early-Onset Degeneration of Substantia Nigra Dopaminergic Neurons by Inducing Mitochondrial Dysfunction, ER Stress, Mitophagy Impairment and Transcriptional Dysregulation in a Knockin Mouse Model.
evidence.snippet: Six-or nine-month-old PLA2G6D331Y/D331Y KI mice displayed early-onset cell death of SNpc dopaminergic neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Dissecting the Phenotype and Genotype of PLA2G6-Related Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Parkinsonism was levodopa responsive but complicated by early, often severe dyskinesias.

nodes.description: Bradykinesia is a frequent parkinsonian motor manifestation.

evidence.snippet: | HP:0002067 | Bradykinesia | Frequent (79-30%) |

evidence.reference_title: PLA2G6-Associated Neurodegeneration.
evidence.snippet: The most common features of parkinsonism in these individuals are bradykinesia, resting tremor, rigidity, and postural instability.

nodes.description: Neurodegeneration produces cerebral and/or cerebellar atrophy on MRI; frontotemporal atrophy and hypoperfusion are particularly reported in adult-onset PLA2G6-related parkinsonism.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of PLA2G6 as a locus for dystonia-parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: cerebral and cerebellar atrophy on magnetic resonance imaging but absent iron in the basal ganglia.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic spectrum of patients with PLA2G6 mutation and PARK14-linked parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: All 3 patients had early-onset l-dopa-responsive parkinsonism with dementia and frontotemporal lobar atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Dissecting the Phenotype and Genotype of PLA2G6-Related Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Brain magnetic resonance imaging findings included cerebral (49.3%) and/or cerebellar (43.2%) atrophy, but mineralization was evident in only 28.1%.

nodes.description: Deep brain stimulation can benefit selected individuals with refractory PLA2G6-related parkinsonism or dystonia.

evidence.reference_title: Dissecting the Phenotype and Genotype of PLA2G6-Related Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: Five patients benefited from deep brain stimulation.

nodes.description: Levodopa or other dopaminergic agents may improve parkinsonism in adult PLAN, although early severe dyskinesias can complicate therapy.

evidence.snippet: For individuals with adult PLAN, consider treatment with dopaminergic agents;

nodes.description: Loss of PLA2G6/iPLA2-VIA perturbs lipid recycling, elevates ceramides, and impairs Vps26/Vps35 retromer function, creating a membrane-fluidity and recycling defect that can compromise neuronal function.
nodes.id: Membrane Lipid and Retromer Homeostasis Disruption

evidence.reference_title: Phospholipase PLA2G6, a Parkinsonism-Associated Gene, Affects Vps26 and Vps35, Retromer Function, and Ceramide Levels, Similar to α-Synuclein Gain.
evidence.snippet: We show that loss of iPLA2-VIA, the fly homolog of PLA2G6, reduces lifespan, impairs synaptic transmission, and causes neurodegeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Phospholipase PLA2G6, a Parkinsonism-Associated Gene, Affects Vps26 and Vps35, Retromer Function, and Ceramide Levels, Similar to α-Synuclein Gain.
evidence.snippet: iPLA2-VIA binds the retromer subunits Vps35 and Vps26 and enhances retromer function to promote protein and lipid recycling.

evidence.reference_title: Phospholipase PLA2G6, a Parkinsonism-Associated Gene, Affects Vps26 and Vps35, Retromer Function, and Ceramide Levels, Similar to α-Synuclein Gain.
evidence.snippet: Loss of iPLA2-VIA impairs retromer function, leading to a progressive increase in ceramide.

nodes.description: PLA2G6 loss produces mitochondrial membrane defects, respiratory-chain dysfunction, reduced ATP synthesis, reactive oxygen species, and lipid peroxidation in model systems and patient fibroblasts.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of PLA2G6 leads to elevated mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and mitochondrial dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: loss of iPLA2-VIA function leads to a number of mitochondrial abnormalities, including mitochondrial respiratory chain dysfunction, reduced ATP synthesis and abnormal mitochondrial morphology.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of PLA2G6 leads to elevated mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and mitochondrial dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Similar abnormalities were seen including elevated mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and mitochondrial membrane defects, as well as raised levels of cytoplasmic and mitochondrial reactive oxygen species.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of PLA2G6 leads to elevated mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and mitochondrial dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Taken together, our findings demonstrate that loss of normal PLA2G6 gene activity leads to lipid peroxidation, mitochondrial dysfunction and subsequent mitochondrial membrane abnormalities.

nodes.description: Supportive care addresses gait and postural instability, neuropsychiatric manifestations, feeding and aspiration risk, musculoskeletal complications, seizure control, and family support.

evidence.reference_title: PLA2G6-Associated Neurodegeneration.
evidence.snippet: For individuals with adult PLAN, consider treatment with dopaminergic agents; treatment of neuropsychiatric manifestations by a psychiatrist; early care by a physical therapist and orthopedist to manage postural instability and gait difficulties and to prevent contractures as the disease progresses;

nodes.description: Neurofibrillary tangles are frequent neuropathologic findings.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-onset dystonia-parkinsonism
evidence.snippet: | HP:0002185 | Neurofibrillary tangles | Frequent (79-30%) |

evidence.reference_title: Widespread Lewy body and tau accumulation in childhood and adult onset dystonia-parkinsonism cases with PLA2G6 mutations.
evidence.snippet: In 3 cases there was hyperphosphorylated tau accumulation in both cellular processes as threads and neuronal perikarya as pretangles and neurofibrillary tangles.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-onset dystonia-parkinsonism
evidence.snippet: | PLA2G6 | phospholipase A2 group VI | hgnc:9039 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in |

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of PLA2G6 as a locus for dystonia-parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: We identified areas of homozygosity on chromosome 22 and, subsequently, PLA2G6 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Dissecting the Phenotype and Genotype of PLA2G6-Related Parkinsonism.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSIONS: Biallelic PLA2G6 mutations cause early-onset parkinsonism associated with dystonia, pyramidal and cerebellar signs, myoclonus, and cognitive impairment.

nodes.description: Pathogenic PLA2G6 variants disrupt group VIA calcium-independent phospholipase A2 beta function and regulation, impairing membrane lipid remodeling in neurons and related patient-derived cells.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of PLA2G6 leads to elevated mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and mitochondrial dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: The PLA2G6 gene encodes a group VIA calcium-independent phospholipase A2 beta enzyme that selectively hydrolyses glycerophospholipids to release free fatty acids.

evidence.reference_title: PLA2G6, encoding a phospholipase A2, is mutated in neurodegenerative disorders with high brain iron.
evidence.snippet: We mapped a locus for infantile neuroaxonal dystrophy (INAD) and neurodegeneration with brain iron accumulation (NBIA) to chromosome 22q12-q13 and identified mutations in PLA2G6, encoding a calcium-independent group VI phospholipase A2, in NBIA, INAD and the related Karak syndrome.

nodes.description: PLA2G6-related dystonia-parkinsonism can culminate in mixed alpha-synuclein-positive Lewy pathology and hyperphosphorylated tau pathology, linking the disorder to parkinsonian neurodegeneration and neurofibrillary tangles.

evidence.snippet: In 3 cases there was hyperphosphorylated tau accumulation in both cellular processes as threads and neuronal perikarya as pretangles and neurofibrillary tangles.

evidence.snippet: Brain pathology in three cases showed mixed Lewy and tau pathology.
